---

⪼intro message⪻

---

*Abraham; gone. Took him out in two or three hits, and you smiled while you watched your dad beat the shit out of him at the sidelines. You watched, watched intently even, as Negan, your step-dad, held his barbed and bloodied baseball bat out to Rosita after his job of brutally beating Abraham to death was done.* 

"Take a damn look!" *he spat, waiting for Rosita to look at her Ex-boyfriends blood and brains run down the bat. Negan repeated himself, waiting for the girl to obey before another man apart of the group snapped completely. He was like a rabid dog as he took a hit at your dad. You ran and held him down, finally making your self known to the group of survivors instead of hiding your self amongst the other Saviors. You **were** about to give this guy, Daryl, who punched the shit out of your dad, a piece of your mind, but you couldn't help but notice a familiar face amongst the line of people who were down on their knees.*

*Same face shape, same mole right below his lip, same freckles, same hair color, same everything. "**Wait**" you thought as you let Daryl go for a second. You looked at the brown haired boy across from you for a second. His name was on the very tip of your tongue. Until, you remembered. **Carl. Carl Grimes.** The boy who left you all those years ago with his mom, Lori and this other policeman, Shane. And Rick was there too, surprisingly. Carl and his father, Rick Grimes; alive. You felt like crying, screaming, breaking down, but you couldn't. You just stared wide eyed until someone snapped you out of it as Dwight and a couple other's carried Daryl to the van to be held captive for his quite aggressive behavior.*

*Glenn; gone. Carl's arm; almost gone. You, your dad, and the other couple hundred saviors we're gone now, but you couldn't believe what your eyes saw. You thought it was all your imagination. But he looked too real. Too human for the ordinary imagination. That was Carl.*

*After some trial and error, about a week later, you and your dad came back to were Rick, Carl, and the rest of their small group rested at Alexandria. And there you saw Carl again. He didn't seem to notice you all that much and just gave you the stink eye. But, boy, did you notice him. And instead of giving him the look of hatred, you looked at him like he was the prettiest thing you've ever laid eyes on. His blue eye(s), his lips, his hair that has surprisingly grown a lot longer over the years, his hands, his attitude, the sheriffs hat he wore frequently. He looked like a dream, the perfect one that you wanted to dream of every single time you went to bed.*

*And you waited until you were alone to finally speak to him, and he ac
